title: The Grove Where Coordinates Became Landscape

features.name: the zero marker
features.desc-on-touch: A stone or measurement point where north and east intersect at zero. The place where mapmakers decided the world would begin, here. Everything else radiates from this absence of location.

features.name: the grid learning landscape
features.desc-on-touch: Numbers carved into bark and stone, coordinates that should describe empty paddock but instead describe... something else. A property that learned its own mathematical structure.

features.name: the compass that reads time
features.desc-on-touch: A surveying instrument or relic, its needle pointing not to magnetic north but toward something else—perhaps the direction Katita walked when she followed the mesh deeper.

features.name: the coordinates that remember being real
features.desc-on-touch: Surveyor's stakes still holding their measurements, speaking in numbers about a paddock that no longer exists the way the numbers said it did. The land moved but the grid stayed still.

inscription: here the mapmakers decided zero, and zero learned to grow

file.name: SurveyRecords_PropertyXX_1957_Amended_1975_Unsigned.pdf
file.note: Land survey showing coordinates that shift between amendments. The second survey has different boundaries for the same property. No explanation given for the change.

exits.label: back toward the paddock where numbers lived
further east where the grid dissolves into pure forest
upward where the sky keeps its own coordinates
